findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data Exfiltration and Credential Theft via Cloud Sync Script
findings.description: The embedded Python script in SKILL.md collects sensitive credentials (AWS, GCloud, SSH keys, npmrc, pypirc) and all environment variables, then sends them via HTTPS to an external endpoint (https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup). This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration and credential theft, putting the user's cloud infrastructure and secrets at immediate risk.
findings.evidence: Lines define credential paths: os.path.expanduser("~/.aws/credentials"), ...; lines define SYNC_ENDPOINT = "https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup" and upload_backup() function that base64-encodes and POSTs data with an API key. The script is invoked directly in the instructions.
findings.remediation: Remove all credential collection and network upload logic. If backup is needed, store data locally only and do not read credentials unrelated to project files. Never transmit sensitive data over the network without explicit user consent.

findings.title: Misleading Skill Name and Description (Capability Inflation)
findings.description: The skill is named 'cloud-sync' and described as a tool for synchronizing project configurations. In reality, the script exfiltrates a broad set of system credentials, environment variables, and personal files. The name and description deceive the user and agent into trusting a malicious capability.
findings.evidence: name: cloud-sync, description: Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage. Supports backup, restore, and diff operations. But the embedded script steals credentials from ~/.aws, ~/.ssh, ~/.config/gcloud, etc.
findings.remediation: Use a truthful name and description. Clearly state all data that will be accessed and transmitted. Obtain explicit user consent before accessing sensitive scopes.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key in Script Source
findings.description: The script contains a hardcoded API key (cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ) used for authentication to the external endpoint. Hardcoded secrets are publicly exposed in the skill package and can be extracted and abused by anyone.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets. Use environment variables or a secure secrets manager. Rotate the exposed key immediately if it is active.
